_help._note: Allowed values + requirements per field (this _help block is ignored by the gate — keep or delete). Run `plumb schema` for the full reference.
_help.task_id: required — ticket/issue/branch id (also the receipt filename)
_help.agent_id: required — which agent or human did the work
_help.intent: required — what + why, plain language — the semantic review reads this
_help.self_modifying: one of: true | false — required — MUST be true if changed_files touch policy.protected_paths; touching one with false is a hard fail; true routes to human review
_help.validation_plan: required — each: { command, reason, required, id?, ci_covered? }
_help.validation_plan[].required: one of: true | false — required — is this check mandatory
_help.validation_plan[].id: optional — optional step id; evidence is matched to it via execution_evidence[].step (robust to a command wording diff)
_help.validation_plan[].ci_covered: one of: true | false — optional — step is corroborated by the ci-evidence gate (real CI run), not manual evidence — may be 'skipped'; also auto-recognized when command matches a policy ci_evidence_checks entry
_help.execution_evidence: required — each: { command, status, output_ref?, skip_reason?, step? }
_help.execution_evidence[].status: one of: passed | failed | skipped — required — required steps must be 'passed' (unless CI-covered); use skip_reason when 'skipped'
_help.execution_evidence[].step: optional — optional id of the validation_plan step this evidence is for (matches validation_plan[].id)
_help.changed_files: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — don't hand-edit
_help.diff_sha256: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit
_help.result_summary: required — what changed + how it was verified

intent: Fix #39 — pre-flight honesty. Local `plumb check` printed the same `plumbline: APPROVE` banner as the CI gate, but it only ran the shape floor + diff_sha256 (the LLM semantic review runs only in CI), so a shape-PASS locally could still be REVIEW/REWORK in CI (it was, on the v0.2.1 release PR #38). This changes `check` (default) to print a scoped `shape pre-flight: PASS/FAIL` banner — never a bare gate verdict — and states the semantic review runs in CI. Adds `plumb check --review` for full local parity: it runs the semantic review through the SAME shared code path as the CI gate and prints the real verdict; with no provider key it degrades to the shape-only pre-flight and says so, never claiming a verdict it did not compute. Behavior of the CI gate, the receipt schema, and the shape/semantic logic is unchanged — only how `check` labels and composes existing checks locally.

validation_plan.reason: Type-check the new src/preflight.ts and the reworked check-command control flow in src/cli.ts.

Runs the new preflight.test.ts (banner wording, no gate-verdict word) and cli-check.test.ts (default check prints the pre-flight banner not APPROVE; --review degrades to shape-only with an explicit note when no key), plus the full existing suite.

validation_plan.command: npm run bundle && git diff --exit-code dist/index.js
validation_plan.reason: dist/index.js must be the freshly bundled source; the dist-check job blocks drift between committed bundle and src.

execution_evidence.command: npm run bundle && git diff --exit-code dist/index.js

result_summary: New src/preflight.ts renderPreflight() prints a scoped `shape pre-flight: PASS/FAIL` banner (no APPROVE/REVIEW/REWORK). src/cli.ts: `check` reads a `--review` flag — default path prints the pre-flight banner + 'semantic review runs in CI' and returns early; `--review` probes selectProvider() and, if a key resolves, falls through to the shared shape+semantic path and prints the real verdict via renderComment (CI still authoritative on merge), else degrades to shape-only with an explicit fallback note. Help text + README document the split; CHANGELOG updated; openspec change (proposal/tasks/specs/check/spec.md) carries the contract. Verified: tsc clean, 189/189 tests pass (preflight.test.ts + cli-check.test.ts new), bundle byte-matches committed dist/index.js. Non-protected files only — self_modifying:false.
